1.arabic: بِسْمِ ٱللَّهِ ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنِ ٱلرَّحِيمِ وَٱلنَّجْمِ إِذَا هَوَىٰ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: I CALL TO witness the star of the pleiades when it has dipped

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: By oath of the beloved shining star Mohammed (peace and blessings be upon him), when he returned from the Ascent.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: By the Star when it plunges,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: By the star when it setteth.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: By the star when it goes down, (or vanishes).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: By the star as it goes down.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: By the star when it sets:

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: By the star when it goes down.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: By the Star when it setteth,

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: By the star when it sets:

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: By the star when it plunges,

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: By the star when it descends,

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: By the declining star,

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: I swear by the star when it goes down.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: By the setting star,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: By the Star when it goes down,-

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Waalnnajmi itha hawa

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: That your companion is not confused, nor has he gone astray,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Your companion did not err, nor did he go astray.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: your comrade is not astray, neither errs,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Your companion hath not gone astray, nor hath he erred.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Your companion (Muhammad SAW) has neither gone astray nor has erred.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Your friend has not gone astray, nor has he erred.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: your companion has neither strayed nor is he deluded;

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Your companion has neither gone astray nor has he erred.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Your comrade erreth not, nor is deceived;

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: your companion has neither gone astray, nor amiss.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: your companion is neither astray, neither errs,

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Your companion [Muhammad] has not strayed, nor has he erred,

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: your companion is not in error nor has he gone astray.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Your companion does not err, nor does he go astray;

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: your companion has neither strayed nor is he misguided,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Your Companion is neither astray nor being misled.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Ma dalla sahibukum wama ghawa

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Neither does he speak of his own will.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And he does not say anything by his own desire.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: nor speaks he out of caprice.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And he speaketh not of his own desire.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Nor does he speak of (his own) desire.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Nor does he speak out of desire.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: nor does he speak out of his desire.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Nor does he speak of desire.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Nor doth he speak of (his own) desire.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Nor does he speak out of [his own] desire:

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: nor does he speak out of desire.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Nor does he speak from [his own] inclination.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: He does not speak out of his own desires.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Nor does he speak out of desire.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: nor does he speak out of his own desire.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Nor does he say (aught) of (his own) Desire.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wama yantiqu AAani alhawa

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: This is only revelation communicated,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: It is but a divine revelation, which is revealed to him.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: This is naught but a revelation revealed,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: It is but a revelation revealed.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: It is only an Inspiration that is inspired.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: It is but a revelation revealed.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: This is nothing but a revelation that is conveyed to him,

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: It is only a revelation revealed.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: It is naught save an inspiration that is inspired,

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: it is just a revelation that is revealed [to him],

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Indeed it is not except a Revelation which is revealed,

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: It is not but a revelation revealed,

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: It is a revelations which has been revealed to him

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: It is naught but revelation that is revealed,

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: It [the Quran] is nothing but revelation sent down to him.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: It is no less than inspiration sent down to him:

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: In huwa illa wahyun yooha

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Bestowed on him by the Supreme Intellect,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: He has been taught by the Extremely Powerful.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: taught him by one terrible in power,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: One of mighty powers hath taught it him.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: He has been taught (this Quran) by one mighty in power [Jibrael (Gabriel)].

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Taught to him by the Extremely Powerful.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: something that a very powerful one has imparted to him,

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: He has been taught by one mighty in power.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Which one of mighty powers hath taught him,

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: taught him by one of great powers,

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: taught by One who is Stern in power.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Taught to him by one intense in strength -

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: and taught to him

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: The Lord of Mighty Power has taught him,

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: He was taught by [an angel] who is mighty in power,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: He was taught by one Mighty in Power,

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: AAallamahu shadeedu alquwa

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Lord of power and wisdom. So he acquired poise and balance,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: The Strong; then the Spectacle inclined towards him.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: very strong; he stood poised,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: One strong of make. Then he stood straight.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Dhu Mirrah (free from any defect in body and mind), Fastawa [then he (Jibrael - Gabriel) rose and became stable]. [Tafsir At-Tabari].

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: The one of vigor. He settled.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: one endowed with immense wisdom. He came forth and stood poised,

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Dhu Mirrah then he Istawa.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: One vigorous; and he grew clear to view

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: possessed of sound judgement. He settled,

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Of might, he (Gabriel) stood firm

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: One of soundness. And he rose to [his] true form

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: by the great mighty one (Gabriel),

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: The Lord of Strength; so he attained completion,

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: and endowed with wisdom; who in time manifested himself;

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Endued with Wisdom: for he appeared (in stately form);

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Thoo mirratin faistawa

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: And reached the highest pinnacle.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And he was on the horizon of the highest heaven.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: being on the higher horizon,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: While he was on the uppermost horizon.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: While he [Jibrael (Gabriel)] was in the highest part of the horizon,

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: While he was at the highest horizon.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: being on the higher horizon.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: While he was in the highest part of the horizon,

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: When he was on the uppermost horizon.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: while he was on the highest horizon.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: while he was in the highest horizon;

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: While he was in the higher [part of the] horizon.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: the strong one who appeared on the uppermost horizon.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And he is in the highest part of the horizon.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: standing poised at the highest point on the horizon,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: While he was in the highest part of the horizon:

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wahuwa bialofuqi alaAAla

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Then he drew near and drew closer

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Then the Spectacle became closer, and came down in full view.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: then drew near and suspended hung,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Thereafter he drew nigh, then he let himself down.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Then he [Jibrael (Gabriel)] approached and came closer,

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Then he came near, and hovered around.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Then he drew near and hung above suspended,

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Then he approached and came closer,

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Then he drew nigh and came down

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Then he drew nearer and nearer

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: then he drew near, and became close

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Then he approached and descended

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: He (Gabriel) then came nearer and nearer.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Then he drew near, then he bowed

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: then came down close

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Then he approached and came closer,

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Thumma dana fatadalla

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Until a space of two bow (arcs) or even less remained,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: So the distance between the Spectacle and the beloved was only two arms’ length, or even less. (The Heavenly Journey of Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him – was with body and soul.)

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: two bows' length away, or nearer,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Till he was two bows length off or yet nearer.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And was at a distance of two bows' length or (even) nearer,

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: He was within two bows’ length, or closer.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: until he was two bows' length away, or nearer.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And was at a distance of two bows' length or less.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Till he was (distant) two bows' length or even nearer,

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: until he was within two bows’ length or even nearer,

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: he was but two bows' length or even nearer,

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And was at a distance of two bow lengths or nearer.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: until he was as close to him as the distance of two bows, or even less.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: So he was the measure of two bows or closer still.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: until he was two bow-lengths away or even closer

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And was at a distance of but two bow-lengths or (even) nearer;

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Fakana qaba qawsayni aw adna

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: When He revealed to His votary what He revealed.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: So Allah divinely revealed to His bondman, whatever He divinely revealed.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: then revealed to his servant that he revealed.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Thus He revealed Unto His bondman whatsoever He revealed.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: So did (Allah) convey the Inspiration to His slave [Muhammad SAW through Jibrael (Gabriel)].

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Then He revealed to His servant what He revealed.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Then he revealed to Allah's servant whatever he had to reveal.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: So (Allah) revealed to His servant whatever He revealed.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And He revealed unto His slave that which He revealed.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: whereat He revealed to His servant whatever He revealed.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: so (Allah) revealed to His worshiper (Gabriel) that which he revealed (to Prophet Muhammad).

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And he revealed to His Servant what he revealed.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: He revealed to God's servant whatever he wanted.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And He revealed to His servant what He revealed.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: and revealed to God's servant what he revealed.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: So did (Allah) convey the inspiration to His Servant- (conveyed) what He (meant) to convey.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Faawha ila AAabdihi ma awha

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: His heart did not falsify what he perceived.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: The heart did not deny, what it saw. (The Holy Prophet was bestowed with seeing Allah – see also preceding verses 8, 9, 10.)

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: His heart lies not of what he saw;

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: The heart lied not in that which he saw.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: The (Prophet's) heart lied not (in seeing) what he (Muhammad SAW) saw.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: The heart did not lie about what it saw.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: His heart added no untruth to what he saw.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: The heart lied not in what he saw.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: The heart lied not (in seeing) what it saw.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: The heart did not deny what it saw.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: His heart did not lie of what he saw.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: The heart did not lie [about] what it saw.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: His (Muhammad's) heart did not lie to him about what his eyes had seen.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: The heart was not untrue in (making him see) what he saw.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: The heart [of the Prophet] did not misconstrue what he saw.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: The (Prophet's) (mind and) heart in no way falsified that which he saw.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Ma kathaba alfuadu ma raa

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Will you dispute with him what he saw?

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: What! So do you dispute with him regarding what he saw?

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: what, will you dispute with him what he sees?

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Will ye therefore dispute with him concerning that which he hath Seen?

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Will you then dispute with him (Muhammad SAW) about what he saw [during the Mi'raj: (Ascent of the Prophet SAW over the seven heavens)].

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Will you dispute with him concerning what he saw?

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Are you, then, going to contend with him regarding what he sees with his eyes?

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Will you then dispute with him about what he saw

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Will ye then dispute with him concerning what he seeth?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Will you then dispute with him about what he saw?!

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: What, will you dispute with him about what he sees!

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: So will you dispute with him over what he saw?

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Will you then argue with him about what he saw?

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: What! do you then dispute with him as to what he saw?

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Will you then dispute with him as to what he saw?

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Will ye then dispute with him concerning what he saw?

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Afatumaroonahu AAala ma yara

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: He saw Him indeed another time

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And indeed he did see the Spectacle again.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Indeed, he saw him another time

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And assuredly he saw him at another descent.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And indeed he (Muhammad SAW) saw him [Jibrael (Gabriel)] at a second descent (i.e. another time).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: He saw him on another descent.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Indeed he saw him a second time,

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And indeed he saw him at a second descent.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And verily he saw him yet another time

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Certainly he saw it yet another time,

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Indeed, he saw him in another descent

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And he certainly saw him in another descent

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: He certainly saw him (Gabriel) during his other ascent

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And certainly he saw him in another descent,

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: And certainly he saw him descend a second time:

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: For indeed he saw him at a second descent,

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Walaqad raahu nazlatan okhra

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: By the Lote-tree beyond which none can pass,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Near the lote-tree of the last boundary.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: by the Lote-Tree of the Boundary

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Nigh Unto the lote-tree at the boundary.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Near Sidrat-ul-Muntaha [lote-tree of the utmost boundary (beyond which none can pass)],

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: At the Lotus Tree of the Extremity.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: by the lote-tree at the farthest boundary,

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Near Sidrat Al-Muntaha.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: By the lote-tree of the utmost boundary,

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: by the Lote Tree of the Ultimate Boundary,

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: at the Lote Tree (Sidrat tree) of the ending

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: At the Lote Tree of the Utmost Boundary -

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: to the Lot-tree (in the seven heavens)

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: At the farthest lote-tree;

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: by the lote tree beyond which none may pass

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Near the Lote-tree beyond which none may pass:

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: AAinda sidrati almuntaha

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Close to which is the Garden of Tranquility,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Close to which is the Everlasting Paradise.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: nigh which is the Garden of the Refuge,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Nigh thereto is the Garden of Abode.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Near it is the Paradise of Abode.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Near which is the Garden of Repose.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: near which is the Garden of Abode.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Near it is the Paradise of Abode.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Nigh unto which is the Garden of Abode.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: near which is the Garden of the Abode,

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: close to the Garden of Refuge.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Near it is the Garden of Refuge -

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: near which is Paradise.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Near which is the garden, the place to be resorted to.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: by the Garden of [Eternal] Repose,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Near it is the Garden of Abode.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: AAindaha jannatu almawa

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: When the Lote-tree was covered over with what it was covered over;

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: When the lote-tree was being enveloped, by whatever around it.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: when there covered the Lote-Tree that which covered;

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: When that covered the lote-tree which covered it.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: When that covered the lote-tree which did cover it!

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: As there covered the Lotus Tree what covered it.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: (This was) when the lote-tree was covered with that which covered it.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: When that covered the lote tree which did cover it!

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: When that which shroudeth did enshroud the lote-tree,

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: when there covered the Lote Tree what covered it.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: When there comes to the Lote Tree, that which comes

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: When there covered the Lote Tree that which covered [it].

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: When the tree was covered with a covering,

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: When that which covers covered the lote-tree;

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: when the lote tree was covered in mystic splendour.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Behold, the Lote-tree was shrouded (in mystery unspeakable!)

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Ith yaghsha alssidrata ma yaghsha

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Neither did sight falter nor exceed the bounds.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: The sight did not shift, nor did it cross the limits.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: his eye swerved not; nor swept astray.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: The sight turned not aside, nor it exceeded.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: The sight (of Prophet Muhammad SAW) turned not aside (right or left), nor it transgressed beyond (the) limit (ordained for it).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: The sight did not waver, nor did it exceed.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: His eye did not waver, nor did it stray,

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: The sight turned not aside, nor it transgressed beyond the limit.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: The eye turned not aside nor yet was overbold.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: His gaze did not swerve, nor did it overstep the bounds.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: his eyes did not swerve, nor did they stray

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: The sight [of the Prophet] did not swerve, nor did it transgress [its limit].

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: (Muhammad's) eyes did not deceive him, nor did they lead him to falsehood.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: The eye did not turn aside, nor did it exceed the limit.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: His sight did not waver nor was it unduly bold.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: (His) sight never swerved, nor did it go wrong!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Ma zagha albasaru wama tagha

18.arabic: لَقَدْ رَأَىٰ مِنْ ءَايَـٰتِ رَبِّهِ ٱلْكُبْرَىٰٓ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Indeed he saw some of the greatest signs of His Lord.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Indeed he saw the supreme signs of his Lord.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Indeed, he saw one of the greatest signs of his Lord.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Assuredly he beheld of the sign of his Lord, the greatest.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Indeed he (Muhammad SAW) did see, of the Greatest Signs, of his Lord (Allah).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: He saw some of the Great Signs of his Lord.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: and he certainly saw some of the greatest Signs of His Lord.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Indeed he saw of the greatest Ssgns of his Lord.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Verily he saw one of the greater revelations of his Lord.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Certainly he saw some of the greatest signs of his Lord.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: for indeed he saw one of the greatest signs of his Lord.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: He certainly saw of the greatest signs of his Lord.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: He certainly saw the greatest (signs) of the existence of his Lord.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Certainly he saw of the greatest signs of his Lord.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: He saw some of the greatest signs of his Lord.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: For truly did he see, of the Signs of his Lord, the Greatest!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Laqad raa min ayati rabbihi alkubra

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Have you considered Lat and 'Uzza,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: So did you observe the idols Lat and Uzza?

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Have you considered El-Lat and El-'Uzza

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Have ye then considered Al-Lat and AL-'uzza:

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Have you then considered Al-Lat, and Al-'Uzza (two idols of the pagan Arabs).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Have you considered al-Lat and al-Uzza?

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Have you ever thought about al-Lat and al-Uzza,

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Have you then considered Al-Lat, and Al-`Uzza.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Have ye thought upon Al-Lat and Al-'Uzza

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Have you considered Lat and ‘Uzza?

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: (Among the idols) have you considered allat and al'uzza,

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: So have you considered al-Lat and al-'Uzza?

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: (Can anything as such be considered true) of al-Lat, al-Uzza,

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Have you then considered the Lat and the Uzza,

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Have you really considered al-Lat and al-"Uzza,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Have ye seen Lat. and 'Uzza,

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Afaraaytumu allata waalAAuzza

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: And Manat, the other third (of the pagan deities)?

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And subsequently the third, the Manat?

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: and Manat the third, the other?

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And Manat the other third?

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And Manat (another idol of the pagan Arabs), the other third?

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And Manat, the third one, the other?

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: and about the third deity, al-Manat?

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And Manat, the other third

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And Manat, the third, the other?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: and Manat, the third one?

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: and, another, the third manat?

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And Manat, the third - the other one?

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: and your third idol al-Manat (whom you considered as God's daughters)?

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And Manat, the third, the last?

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: and the third one, Manat? --

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: And another, the third (goddess), Manat?

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wamanata alththalithata alokhra

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Are there sons for you, and daughters for Him?

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: What! For you the son, and for Him the daughter?

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: What, have you males, and He females?

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: What!-Unto YOu the males and Unto Him the females?

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Is it for you the males and for Him the females?

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Are you to have the males, and He the females?

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Shall you have the male issues, and He the female issues?

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Is it for you the males and for Him the females

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Are yours the males and His the females?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Are you to have males and He females?

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: What, have you males, and He females!

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Is the male for you and for Him the female?

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Do sons belong to you and daughters to God?

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: What! for you the males and for Him the females!

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: "What! For you the males and for Him the females?"

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: What! for you the male sex, and for Him, the female?

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Alakumu alththakaru walahu alontha

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: This is certainly an unjust apportioning.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Then that is surely a very unjust distribution!

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: That were indeed an unjust division.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: That indeed is a division unfair!

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: That indeed is a division most unfair!

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: What a bizarre distribution.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: That is indeed an unfair division!

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: That indeed is a division most unfair!

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: That indeed were an unfair division!

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: That, then, will be an unfair division!

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: That is indeed an unjust division.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: That, then, is an unjust division.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: This is an unfair distinction!

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: This indeed is an unjust division!

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: That indeed is an unfair division --

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Behold, such would be indeed a division most unfair!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Tilka ithan qismatun deeza

23.arabic: إِنْ هِىَ إِلَّآ أَسْمَآءٌ سَمَّيْتُمُوهَآ أَنتُمْ وَءَابَآؤُكُم مَّآ أَنزَلَ ٱللَّهُ بِهَا مِن سُلْطَـٰنٍ ۚ إِن يَتَّبِعُونَ إِلَّا ٱلظَّنَّ وَمَا تَهْوَى ٱلْأَنفُسُ ۖ وَلَقَدْ جَآءَهُم مِّن رَّبِّهِمُ ٱلْهُدَىٰٓ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: These are only names which you and your fathers have invented. No authority was sent down by God for them. They only follow conjecture and wish-fulfilment, even though guidance had come to them already from their Lord.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: They are nothing but some names that you have coined, you and your forefathers – Allah has not sent any proof for them; they follow only guesses and their own desires; whereas the guidance from their Lord has come to them.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: They are naught but names yourselves have named, and your fathers; God has sent down no authority touching them. They follow only surmise, and what the souls desire; and yet guidance has come to them from their Lord.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: They are but names which ye have named, ye and your fathers, for which Allah hath sent down no authority. They follow but their fancy, and that which pleaseth their souls; whereas assuredly there hath come Unto them from their Lord the guidance.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: They are but names which you have named, you and your fathers, for which Allah has sent down no authority. They follow but a guess and that which they themselves desire, whereas there has surely come to them the Guidance from their Lord!

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: These are nothing but names, which you have devised, you and your ancestors, for which God sent down no authority. They follow nothing but assumptions, and what the ego desires, even though guidance has come to them from their Lord.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: These are nothing but names that you and your forefathers have invented, for which Allah has sent down no authority. They are merely following their conjectures and their carnal desires although guidance has come to them from their Lord.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: They are but names which you have named - you and your fathers - for which Allah has sent down no authority. They follow but a guess and that which they themselves desire, whereas there has surely come to them the guidance from their Lord!

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: They are but names which ye have named, ye and your fathers, for which Allah hath revealed no warrant. They follow but a guess and that which (they) themselves desire. And now the guidance from their Lord hath come unto them.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: These are but names, which you have coined—you and your fathers—for which Allah has not sent down any authority. They follow nothing but conjectures and the desires of the [lower] soul, while there has already come to them the guidance from their Lord.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: They are but names, named by you and your fathers. Allah has not sent down any authority for them. They follow conjecture and their soul's desire, eventhough the guidance of their Lord has come to them.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: They are not but [mere] names you have named them - you and your forefathers - for which Allah has sent down no authority. They follow not except assumption and what [their] souls desire, and there has already come to them from their Lord guidance.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: These are only names given by yourselves and your fathers. God has not given them any authority. They, (unbelievers), only follow mere conjecture and the desires of their souls, even though guidance has already come to them from their Lord.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: They are naught but names which you have named, you and your fathers; Allah has not sent for them any authority. They follow naught but conjecture and the low desires which (their) souls incline to; and certainly the guidance has come to them from their Lord.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: these are nothing but names which you yourselves have devised, you and your forefathers. God has sent down no authority for them. They follow nothing but conjecture and what their own selves desire, even though guidance has already come to them from their Lord!

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: These are nothing but names which ye have devised,- ye and your fathers,- for which Allah has sent down no authority (whatever). They follow nothing but conjecture and what their own souls desire!- Even though there has already come to them Guidance from their Lord!

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: In hiya illa asmaon sammaytumooha antum waabaokum ma anzala Allahu biha min sultanin in yattabiAAoona illa alththanna wama tahwa alanfusu walaqad jaahum min rabbihimu alhuda

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Can ever man get what he desires?

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: What! Will man get whatever he dreams of?

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Or shall man have whatever he fancies?

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Shall man have whatsoever he wisheth for!

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Or shall man have what he wishes?

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Or is the human being to have whatever he desires?

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Does man imagine that whatever he wishes for is right for him?

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Or shall man have what he wishes

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Or shall man have what he coveteth?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Shall man have whatever he yearns for?

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Is the human to have whatever he fancies?

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Or is there for man whatever he wishes?

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Can the human being have whatever he wishes?

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Or shall man have what he wishes?

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Shall man have whatever he craves?

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Nay, shall man have (just) anything he hankers after?

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Am lilinsani ma tamanna

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: To God belong the End and the Beginning.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: So (know that) Allah only is the Owner of all – the Hereafter and this world.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: And to God belongs the First and the Last.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Allah's is the last and the first.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: But to Allah belongs the last (Hereafter) and the first (the world).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: To God belong the Last and the First.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: To Allah belong both the Next World and the present.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: But to Allah belongs the last (Hereafter) and the first (the world).

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: But unto Allah belongeth the after (life), and the former.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Yet to Allah belong this world and the Hereafter.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: To Allah belongs the Eternal life and the first life.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Rather, to Allah belongs the Hereafter and the first [life].

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: All that is in the life to come and all that is in this life belongs only to God.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Nay! for Allah is the hereafter and the former (life).

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: But it is to God that the Hereafter and this world belong.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: But it is to Allah that the End and the Beginning (of all things) belong.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Falillahi alakhiratu waaloola

26.arabic: وَكَم مِّن مَّلَكٍ فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ لَا تُغْنِى شَفَـٰعَتُهُمْ شَيْـًٔا إِلَّا مِنۢ بَعْدِ أَن يَأْذَنَ ٱللَّهُ لِمَن يَشَآءُ وَيَرْضَىٰٓ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Many as the angels be in heaven their intercession will not avail in the least without God's permission for whomsoever He please and approve.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And many an angel is in the heavens, whose intercession does not benefit the least unless Allah gives permission for whomever He wills, and whom He likes.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: How many an angel there is in the heavens whose intercession avails not anything, save after that God gives leave to whomsoever He wills and is well-pleased.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And many soever are angels in the heavens whose intercession shall not avail at all save after Allah hath given leave for whomsoever He listeth and pleaseth.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And there are many angels in the heavens, whose intercession will avail nothing except after Allah has given leave for whom He wills and pleases.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: How many an angel is there in the heavens whose intercession avails nothing, except after God gives permission to whomever He wills, and approves?

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Numerous are the angels in the heavens; yet their intercession shall be of no avail, except in regard to those whom He grants the leave of intercession and whose plea He is pleased to accept.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And there are many angels in the heavens, whose intercession will avail nothing, except after Allah has given leave for whom He wills and is pleased with.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And how many angels are in the heavens whose intercession availeth naught save after Allah giveth leave to whom He chooseth and accepteth.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: How many an angel there is in the heavens whose intercession is of not any avail, except after Allah permits whomever He wishes and approves of!

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: How many an angel is there in the heavens whose intercession shall not benefit until Allah gives permission to whom He will and is pleased.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And how many angels there are in the heavens whose intercession will not avail at all except [only] after Allah has permitted [it] to whom He wills and approves.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: There are many angels in the heavens whose intercession will be of no benefit unless God grants such permission to whichever of them He wants.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And how many an angel is there in the heavens whose intercession does not avail at all except after Allah has given permission to whom He pleases and chooses.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: There may be countless angels in heaven, but their intercession will be of no avail until God has given permission to those whom he chooses and accepts.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: How many-so-ever be the angels in the heavens, their intercession will avail nothing except after Allah has given leave for whom He pleases and that he is acceptable to Him.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wakam min malakin fee alssamawati la tughnee shafaAAatuhum shayan illa min baAAdi an yathana Allahu liman yashao wayarda

27.arabic: إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ لَيُسَمُّونَ ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةَ تَسْمِيَةَ ٱلْأُنثَىٰ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Those who do not believe in the Hereafter give the angels names of females.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Indeed those who do not believe in the Hereafter, coin the names of angels like those of females.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Those who do not believe in the world to come name the angels with the names of females.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Verily those who believe not in the Hereafter name the angels with the names of females.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Verily, those who believe not in the Hereafter, name the angels with female names.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Those who do not believe in the Hereafter give the angels the names of females.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Those who do not believe in the Hereafter give angels the names of females,

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Verily, those who believe not in the Hereafter, name the angels with female names.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Lo! it is those who disbelieve in the Hereafter who name the angels with the names of females.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Indeed those who do not believe in the Hereafter give female names to the angels.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Those who disbelieve in the Everlasting Life call the angels by female names.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Indeed, those who do not believe in the Hereafter name the angels female names,

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Only those who do not believe in the life hereafter call the angels, females. They have no knowledge about it.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Most surely they who do not believe in the hereafter name the angels with female names.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Those who do not believe in the life to come call the angels by female names.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Those who believe not in the Hereafter, name the angels with female names.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Inna allatheena la yuminoona bialakhirati layusammoona almalaikata tasmiyata alontha

28.arabic: وَمَا لَهُم بِهِۦ مِنْ عِلْمٍ ۖ إِن يَتَّبِعُونَ إِلَّا ٱلظَّنَّ ۖ وَإِنَّ ٱلظَّنَّ لَا يُغْنِى مِنَ ٱلْحَقِّ شَيْـًٔا

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Yet they have no knowledge of this, and follow nothing but conjecture, but conjecture cannot replace the truth.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And they do not have any knowledge of it; they just follow assumption; and indeed assumption does not serve any purpose in place of the Truth.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: They have not any knowledge thereof; they follow only surmise, and surmise avails naught against truth.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And they have no knowledge thereof; they follow but a vain opinion, and verily a vain opinion attaineth not aught of the truth.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: While they have no knowledge thereof. They follow but a guess, and verily, guess is no substitute for the truth.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: They have no knowledge of that. They only follow assumptions, and assumptions are no substitute for the truth.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: although they have no knowledge regarding that. They only follow their conjecture and conjecture can never take the place of the Truth.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: But they have no knowledge thereof. They follow but conjecture, and verily, conjecture is no substitute for the truth.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And they have no knowledge thereof. They follow but a guess, and lo! a guess can never take the place of the truth.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: They do not have any knowledge of that. They follow nothing but conjectures, and conjecture is no substitute for the truth.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Yet of this they have no knowledge, they follow mere conjecture, and conjecture does not help against truth.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And they have thereof no knowledge. They follow not except assumption, and indeed, assumption avails not against the truth at all.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: They only follow mere conjecture which can never sufficiently replace the Truth.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And they have no knowledge of it; they do not follow anything but conjecture, and surely conjecture does not avail against the truth at all.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: They have no knowledge to base this on. They merely indulge in guess-work which can never replace the truth.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: But they have no knowledge therein. They follow nothing but conjecture; and conjecture avails nothing against Truth.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Wama lahum bihi min AAilmin in yattabiAAoona illa alththanna wainna alththanna la yughnee mina alhaqqi shayan

29.arabic: فَأَعْرِضْ عَن مَّن تَوَلَّىٰ عَن ذِكْرِنَا وَلَمْ يُرِدْ إِلَّا ٱلْحَيَوٰةَ ٱلدُّنْيَا

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: So you turn away from him who turns away from Our rememberance and wants nothing but the life of this world:

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Therefore turn away from whoever has turned away from Our remembrance and has desired only the life of this world.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: So turn thou from him who turns away from Our Remembrance, and desires only the present life.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Wherefore withdraw thou from him who turneth away from Our admonition and seeketh not but the life of the world.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Therefore withdraw (O Muhammad SAW) from him who turns away from Our Reminder (this Quran) and desires nothing but the life of this world.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: So avoid him who has turned away from Our remembrance, and desires nothing but the present life.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: So leave alone those who turn away from the remembrance of Us and who seek nothing but the life of the world –

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Therefore withdraw from him who turns away from Our Reminder and desires nothing but the life of this world.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Then withdraw (O Muhammad) from him who fleeth from Our remembrance and desireth but the life of the world.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: So avoid those who turn away from Our remembrance and desire nothing but the life of the world.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: So turn from those who turn away from Our Remembrance and only desire this present life.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: So turn away from whoever turns his back on Our message and desires not except the worldly life.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: (Muhammad), stay away from those who turn away from Our guidance and who do not desire anything except the worldly life.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Therefore turn aside from him who turns his back upon Our reminder and does not desire anything but this world's life.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: So ignore those who turn away from Our revelation and seek nothing but the life of this world.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Therefore shun those who turn away from Our Message and desire nothing but the life of this world.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: FaaAArid AAan man tawalla AAan thikrina walam yurid illa alhayata alddunya

30.arabic: ذَٰلِكَ مَبْلَغُهُم مِّنَ ٱلْعِلْمِ ۚ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ هُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِمَن ضَلَّ عَن سَبِيلِهِۦ وَهُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِمَنِ ٱهْتَدَىٰ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: This is the farthest limit of their knowledge. Surely your Lord alone knows best who has strayed away from the path and who has come to guidance.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: This is the extent of their knowledge; indeed your Lord well knows one who has strayed from His path, and He well knows one who has attained guidance.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: That is their attainment of knowledge. Surely thy Lord knows very well those who have gone astray from His way, and He knows very well those who are guided.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: That is their highest point of knowledge. Verily thine Lord! it is He Who is the Best Knower of him who strayeth from His way, and He is the Best Knower of him who letteth himself be guided.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: That is what they could reach of knowledge. Verily, your Lord it is He Who knows best him who goes astray from His Path, and He knows best him who receives guidance.

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: That is the extent of their knowledge. Your Lord knows best who has strayed from His path, and He knows best who has accepted guidance.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: that being the utmost of their knowledge. Surely your Lord fully knows those who have strayed away from His Path and He also fully knows those who are rightly guided.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: That is what they could reach of knowledge. Verily, your Lord is He, Who knows best him who goes astray from His path, and He knows best him who receives guidance.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Such is their sum of knowledge. Lo! thy Lord is Best Aware of him who strayeth, and He is Best Aware of him whom goeth right.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: That is the ultimate reach of their knowledge. Indeed your Lord knows best those who stray from His way, and He knows best those who are [rightly] guided.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: That is all they have of knowledge. Your Lord knows best who has strayed from His Path, and those who are guided.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: That is their sum of knowledge. Indeed, your Lord is most knowing of who strays from His way, and He is most knowing of who is guided.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: This is what the extent of their knowledge amounts to. Your Lord knows best who has gone astray from His path and who has been rightly guided.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: That is their goal of knowledge; surely your Lord knows best him who goes astray from His path and He knows best him who follows the right direction.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: That is the ultimate extent of their knowledge. Surely your Lord knows best those who stray from His path and He knows best those who follow His guidance.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: That is as far as knowledge will reach them. Verily thy Lord knoweth best those who stray from His Path, and He knoweth best those who receive guidance.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Thalika mablaghuhum mina alAAilmi inna rabbaka huwa aAAlamu biman dalla AAan sabeelihi wahuwa aAAlamu bimani ihtada

31.arabic: وَلِلَّهِ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَمَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ لِيَجْزِىَ ٱلَّذِينَ أَسَـٰٓـُٔوا۟ بِمَا عَمِلُوا۟ وَيَجْزِىَ ٱلَّذِينَ أَحْسَنُوا۟ بِٱلْحُسْنَى

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: To God belongs whatever is in the heavens and the earth, that He may requite those who do evil, in accordance with their deeds, and those who do good with good.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And to Allah only belongs all whatever is in the heavens and all whatever is in the earth, in order to repay those who do evil for what they have done, and give an excellent reward to those who do good.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: To God belongs whatsoever is in the heavens and whatsoever is in the earth, that He may recompense those who do evil for what they have done, and recompense those who have done good with the reward most fair.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And Allah's is whatsoever is in the heavens and whatsoever is in the earth, that He may recompense those who do evil for that which they worked and reward those who do good with good.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And to Allah belongs all that is in the heavens and all that is in the earth, that He may requite those who do evil with that which they have done (i.e. punish them in Hell), and reward those who do good, with what is best (i.e. Paradise).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: To God belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on earth. He will repay those who do evil according to their deeds, and recompense those who do good with the best.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: To Allah alone belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is in the earth. He will requite the evil-doers for their deeds and bestow a goodly reward on those who have done good,

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And to Allah belongs all that is in the heavens and all that is on the earth, that He may requite those who do evil with that which they have done, and reward those who do good, with what is best.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And unto Allah belongeth whatsoever is in the heavens and whatsoever is in the earth, that He may reward those who do evil with that which they have done, and reward those who do good with goodness.

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: To Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is in the earth, that He may requite those who do evil for what they have done, and reward those who do good with the best [of rewards].

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: To Allah belongs whatsoever is in the heavens and whatsoever is in the earth. He will recompense the evildoers according to their deeds, and recompense those who have done good with the finest reward

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And to Allah belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is in the earth - that He may recompense those who do evil with [the penalty of] what they have done and recompense those who do good with the best [reward] -

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: To God belongs whatever is in the heavens and the earth. In the end God will re-compense the evil doers for their deeds and reward the righteous ones for their deeds.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And Allah's is what is in the heavens and what is in the earth, that He may reward those who do evil according to what they do, and (that) He may reward those who do good with goodness.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Everything in the heavens and on the earth belongs to God and so He will requite those who do evil in accordance with their deeds and will reward those left with that which is best, for those who do good.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Yea, to Allah belongs all that is in the heavens and on earth: so that He rewards those who do evil, according to their deeds, and He rewards those who do good, with what is best.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Walillahi ma fee alssamawati wama fee alardi liyajziya allatheena asaoo bima AAamiloo wayajziya allatheena ahsanoo bialhusna

32.arabic: ٱلَّذِينَ يَجْتَنِبُونَ كَبَـٰٓئِرَ ٱلْإِثْمِ وَٱلْفَوَٰحِشَ إِلَّا ٱللَّمَمَ ۚ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ وَٰسِعُ ٱلْمَغْفِرَةِ ۚ هُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِكُمْ إِذْ أَنشَأَكُم مِّنَ ٱلْأَرْضِ وَإِذْ أَنتُمْ أَجِنَّةٌ فِى بُطُونِ أُمَّهَـٰتِكُمْ ۖ فَلَا تُزَكُّوٓا۟ أَنفُسَكُمْ ۖ هُوَ أَعْلَمُ بِمَنِ ٱتَّقَىٰٓ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: As for those who avoid the greater sins and shameful acts, except minor trespasses, your Lord's forgiveness surely has great amplitude. He is fully knowledgeable of you as He produced you from the earth, and since you were a foetus in your mother's womb. So do not assert your goodness; he is better who takes heed and preserves himself.

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Those who avoid the cardinal sins and lewdness, except that they approached it and refrained; indeed your Lord’s mercy is limitless; He knows you very well – since He has created you from clay, and when you were foetuses in your mothers’ wombs; therefore do not, on your own, claim yourselves to be clean; He well knows who are the pious.

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Those who avoid the heinous sins and indecencies, save lesser offences surely thy Lord is wide in His forgiveness. Very well He knows you, when He produced you from the earth, and when you were yet unborn in your mothers' wombs; therefore hold not yourselves purified; God knows very well him who is godfearing.

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: They are those who avoid enormities of sin and abominations save the minor offences. Verily thy Lord is of vast for giveness: He is Best Knower of you when He produced you out Of the earth, and when ye were embryos in the bellies of your mothers. So justify not yourselves; He is the Best Knower of him who feareth Him.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Those who avoid great sins (see the Quran, Verses: 6:152, 153) and Al-Fawahish (illegal sexual intercourse, etc.) except the small faults, verily, your Lord is of vast forgiveness. He knows you well when He created you from the earth (Adam), and when you were fetuses in your mothers' wombs. So ascribe not purity to yourselves. He knows best him who fears Allah and keep his duty to Him [i.e. those who are Al-Muttaqun (pious - see V. 2:2)].

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Those who avoid gross sins and indecencies—except for minor lapses—your Lord is of Vast Forgiveness. He knows you well, ever since He created you from the earth, and ever since you were embryos in your mothers’ wombs. So do not acclaim your own virtue; He is fully aware of the righteous.

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: on those who avoid grave sins and shameful deeds, even if they may sometimes stumble into lesser offences. Surely your Lord is abounding in His Forgiveness. Very well is He aware of you since He produced you from the earth, and while you were still in your mothers' wombs and not yet born. So do not boastfully claim yourselves to be purified. He fully knows those that are truly Godfearing.

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Those who avoid great sins and Al-Fawahish (immoral sins) except Al-Lamam -- verily, your Lord is of vast forgiveness. He knows you well when He created you from the earth (Adam), and when you were fetuses in your mothers' wombs. So, ascribe not purity to yourselves. He knows best him who has Taqwa.

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Those who avoid enormities of sin and abominations, save the unwilled offences - (for them) lo! thy Lord is of vast mercy. He is Best Aware of you (from the time) when He created you from the earth, and when ye were hidden in the bellies of your mothers. Therefor ascribe not purity unto yourselves. He is Best Aware of him who wardeth off (evil).

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Those who avoid major sins and indecencies, apart from [minor and occasional] lapses. Indeed your Lord is expansive in [His] forgiveness. He knows you best since [the time] He produced you from the earth and since you were foetuses in the bellies of your mothers. So do not flaunt your piety: He knows best those who are Godwary.

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: those who avoid the major sin and indecencies except the small sins, indeed your Lord is of immense forgiveness and He is more knowledgeable of you when He created you from the earth and when you were still unborn in your mothers' wombs. Do not praise yourself. Allah knows the cautious.

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Those who avoid the major sins and immoralities, only [committing] slight ones. Indeed, your Lord is vast in forgiveness. He was most knowing of you when He produced you from the earth and when you were fetuses in the wombs of your mothers. So do not claim yourselves to be pure; He is most knowing of who fears Him.

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Those who stay away from grave sins and indecency (should know that) for their trivial sins your Lord's forgiveness is vast. He knows best about you. When He created you from the earth and when you were embryos in your mother's wombs. Do not consider yourselves very great. God knows best who is the most righteous person.

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Those who keep aloof from the great sins and the indecencies but the passing idea; surely your Lord is liberal in forgiving. He knows you best when He brings you forth from the earth and when you are embryos in the wombs of your mothers; therefore do not attribute purity to your souls; He knows him best who guards (against evil).

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: As for those who refrain from committing grave sins and indecent acts, though they may commit minor offences, your Lord is unstinting in His forgiveness. He knows you when He brings you out of the earth, and when you were embryos in the wombs of your mothers; so do not make claims to be pure. He knows best who is truly righteous.

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Those who avoid great sins and shameful deeds, only (falling into) small faults,- verily thy Lord is ample in forgiveness. He knows you well when He brings you out of the earth, And when ye are hidden in your mothers' wombs. Therefore justify not yourselves: He knows best who it is that guards against evil.

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Allatheena yajtaniboona kabaira alithmi waalfawahisha illa allamama inna rabbaka wasiAAu almaghfirati huwa aAAlamu bikum ith anshaakum mina alardi waith antum ajinnatun fee butooni ommahatikum fala tuzakkoo anfusakum huwa aAAlamu bimani ittaqa

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Have you seen him who turns his back,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: So did you observe him who turned away?

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Hast thou considered him who turns his back

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Observedest thou him who turned away?

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Did you (O Muhammad SAW) observe him who turned away (from Islam).

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Have you considered him who turned away?

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: (O Prophet), did you see him who turned away (from the Path of Allah),

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Did you observe him who turned away (from Islam).

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Didst thou (O Muhammad) observe him who turned away,

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Did you see him who turned away,

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Have you considered he who turns his back,

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Have you seen the one who turned away

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: (Muhammad), have you ever seen the one who has turned away (from guidance),

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Have you then seen him who turns his back?

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Have you [Prophet] considered the man who turned away,

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Seest thou one who turns back,

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Afaraayta allathee tawalla

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Who gives but little, and is niggardly?

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And he gave a little, then refrained?

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: and gives a little, and then grudgingly?

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And gave little, and then stopped?

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And gave a little, then stopped (giving)?

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: And gave a little, and held back?

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: who gave a little, and then stopped?

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: And gave a little, then stopped

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: And gave a little, then was grudging?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: gave a little and held off?

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: and gives a little, grudgingly?

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: And gave a little and [then] refrained?

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: and grudgingly spends very little for the cause of God?

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: And gives a little and (then) withholds.

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: who at first gave a little, then later held back?

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Gives a little, then hardens (his heart)?

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: WaaAAta qaleelan waakda

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Has he knowledge of the Unknown that he perceives everything?

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Does he have knowledge of the hidden, so he can foresee?

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Does he possess the knowledge of the Unseen, and therefore he sees?

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Is with him knowledge of the unseen so that he seeth?

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Is with him the knowledge of the unseen so that he sees?

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Does he possess knowledge of the unseen, and can therefore foresee?

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Does he have any knowledge of the world beyond the ken of sense-perception, and therefore, clearly sees (the Truth)?

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Is with him the knowledge of the Unseen so that he sees

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Hath he knowledge of the Unseen so that he seeth?

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Does he have the knowledge of the Unseen so that he sees?

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Or, does he possess knowledge of the Unseen, and can therefore see?

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Does he have knowledge of the unseen, so he sees?

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Does he possess the knowledge of the unseen, thus, he sees (all things)?

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Has he the knowledge of the unseen so that he can see?

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Has he knowledge of the unseen, so that he sees?

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: What! Has he knowledge of the Unseen so that he can see?

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: aAAindahu AAilmu alghaybi fahuwa yara

36.arabic: أَمْ لَمْ يُنَبَّأْ بِمَا فِى صُحُفِ مُوسَىٰ

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: Has he not heard what is contained in the Book of Moses,

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: Did not the news reach him, of that which is mentioned in the Books of Moosa?

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: Or has he not been told of what is in the scrolls of Moses,

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: Hath he not been told of that which is in the writs of Musa.

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: Or is he not informed with what is in the Pages (Scripture) of Musa (Moses),

translations.author: Itani
translations.text: Or was he not informed of what is in the Scrolls of Moses?

translations.author: Maududi
translations.text: Has he not been informed of what is in the Scrolls of Moses,

translations.author: Mubarakpuri
translations.text: Or is he not informed with what is in the Suhuf of Musa,

translations.author: Pickthall
translations.text: Or hath he not had news of what is in the books of Moses

translations.author: Qarai
translations.text: Has he not been informed of what is in the scriptures of Moses,

translations.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
translations.text: Or, has he not been told of that which is in the Scrolls of Moses

translations.author: Saheeh International
translations.text: Or has he not been informed of what was in the scriptures of Moses

translations.author: Sarwar
translations.text: Has not he been informed of the contents of the Book of Moses

translations.author: Shakir
translations.text: Or, has he not been informed of what is in the scriptures of Musa?

translations.author: Wahiduddin Khan
translations.text: Has he not been made acquainted with what was written in the scriptures of Moses?

translations.author: Yusuf Ali
translations.text: Nay, is he not acquainted with what is in the Books of Moses-

translations.author: Transliteration
translations.text: Am lam yunabba bima fee suhufi moosa

translations.author: Ahmed Ali
translations.text: And of Abraham who fulfilled his trust? --

translations.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
translations.text: And of Ibrahim, who was most obedient?

translations.author: Arberry
translations.text: and Abraham, he who paid his debt in full?

translations.author: Daryabadi
translations.text: And of Ibrahim who faithfully fulfilled?

translations.author: Hilali & Khan
translations.text: And of Ibrahim (Abraham) who fulfilled (or conveyed) all that (what Allah ordered him to do or convey),

translations.author: Itani
